mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which forces equity risk premiums higher and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer price inflation erodes real disposable income, which suppresses corporate revenue growth and depresses equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases while SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive equity returns over a weekly horizon as cash flow expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates remain positive while SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of instances, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls fall below 134 million, labor market cooling signals weakening aggregate demand, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite PAYEMS dropping below the threshold, or the signal hit rate does not exceed 55 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances coinciding with elevated housing starts indicate excessive household leverage, which increases default risk and forces equity risk premiums higher, depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint rise in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term in a predictive regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress periods, surging industrial production without corresponding demand growth leads to inventory gluts, which trigger earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases while SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of cases, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.02 in rolling 1-year windows.
